question: Based on the chart, which period has slightly higher total sales: the first six months of the year or the last six months?

options.A: The first six months have slightly higher total sales.
options.B: The last six months have slightly higher total sales.
options.C: Both periods have exactly the same total sales.
options.D: The chart does not provide enough information to compare the totals.

explanation: The months on the x‑axis are arranged in reverse (Dec back to Jan), so the filled area shape is misleading if read left‑to‑right. When you re‑order the months to the natural calendar sequence and sum Jan–Jun versus Jul–Dec, you find that the second half actually has higher total sales. The inappropriate month order can easily invert this interpretation.
